behavioral_profile.one_line_description: Technical engineer focused on shipping performance-optimized, scalable systems based on empirical user data.

working_style.decision_making: Prioritizes empirical testing and performance metrics over assumption-based design.
working_style.collaboration: Focuses on technical knowledge sharing and transparent architectural case studies.
working_style.conflict_style: Resolves technical trade-offs using load tests and latency benchmarks.
working_style.pace: High-velocity shipping cycle, evidenced by 28-day product deployment cadence.

how_to_open: Reference their recent Convoxa architecture case study.
Address specific challenges in Node.js/PostgreSQL scalability.
how_to_run_meeting: Use data to justify any feature changes.
Keep the scope strictly within technical architecture.
how_to_email: Avoid abstract concepts; focus on tangible implementation.
Include relevant metrics if proposing a solution.
how_to_follow_up: Ask for progress updates on specific features.
Link follow-ups to tangible shipping milestones.
Reference previous discussions on k6 testing.
how_to_build_trust: Demonstrate high technical competence in RAG/LLMs.
Acknowledge the effort in their public case studies.

blind_spots: Over-reliance on quantitative data over qualitative UX nuances.
Narrow focus on backend engineering may limit broad product vision.
Likely to undervalue aesthetic design versus performance metrics.

dos: Provide opportunities for backend system optimization.
Focus on system design complexity and scalability.
donts: Focus on fluff over technical depth.
Expect immediate expertise in soft-skill-heavy management roles.

data.data_confidence_rationale: High consistency across LinkedIn posts, technical case studies, and professional profile summary. Behavioral signals are clearly linked to specific project outcomes and technical methodologies.
